# Navigation Beacons

Zecharia Sitchin proposed that the Anunnaki established a system of navigation beacons on Earth to guide their spacecraft as they approached and landed on the planet. These beacons took the form of massive stone structures — pyramids, megalithic platforms, and aligned stone circles — that remain some of the most impressive monuments of the ancient world.

## The Beacon System

The navigation beacon system served multiple functions:

1. **Identification** — Identifying the landing site from orbit or high altitude
2. **Alignment** — Providing precise directional references
3. **Guidance** — Marking the approach path to the spaceport
4. **Communication** — Possibly containing communication devices (the [DUR.AN.KI](../concepts/DUR.AN.KI.md))

## The Beacons

### The Great Pyramid (Giza)

The [Great Pyramid](../places/giza.md) was the primary beacon:

- Its original polished limestone casing made it shine like a star
- Its location at the center of Earth's landmass
- Its precise alignment to true north (within 0.05 degrees)
- Its reflective surface visible from great distances
- Its internal chambers aligned to key stars (Orion, Sirius)

### The Baalbek Platform

The megalithic platform at [Baalbek](../places/Baalbek.md) served as both launch pad and beacon:

- Its massive, flat surface was visible from above
- The huge stones (800–1,200 tons) could not have been moved by any conventional ancient technology
- The platform's orientation matches the landing corridor

### The Sphinx

The Sphinx at Giza was the "guardian" of the beacon system:

- Oriented to the rising sun on the equinox
- Possibly built to mark the Age of Leo (c. 10,500 BCE)
- Its position guards the Giza complex

## The Landing Corridor

The beacons formed a precise alignment:

| Landmark | Role |
|----------|------|
| [Ararat](../places/Ararat.md) | Northern approach |
| [Jerusalem](../places/jerusalem.md) | Mount of the Crossing (guidance) |
| [Baalbek](../places/Baalbek.md) | Launch platform |
| [Giza](../places/giza.md) | Primary navigation beacon |
| [Sinai](../places/sinai.md) | Spaceport landing field |

## Other Possible Beacons

Sitchin and other researchers have suggested that many other ancient sites may have served as beacons or observation posts:

- [Stonehenge](../places/Stonehenge.md) — Astronomical observatory
- [Tiwanaku](../places/Tiwanaku.md) — Andean observation post
- Carnac (France) — Megalithic alignments
- Teotihuacan — Mesoamerican complex
- Machu Picchu — Highland observation post
